name: Don Quijote Honolulu (Deli / Poke Counter)
tagline: 24 hours, legit poke, fresh bento, spam musubi — the Japanese-style superstore that locals hit at 2am without shame.
mustOrder: Poke by the container — multiple varieties, always fresh and cheap
Spam musubi — perfectly proportioned rice-to-spam ratio
Bento boxes — Japanese-quality prepared meals at supermarket prices
parking: Large free parking structure attached to the store
talkStory: Part of the Japanese Don Quijote discount chain (this Kaheka St location was formerly a Daiei supermarket), the 24-hour deli counter became a Honolulu institution for its surprisingly excellent poke and prepared foods. The late-night poke run is a local tradition — the counter gets restocked throughout the day so even 2am poke is fresh. Takoyaki and tempura near the deli are sleeper hits. Open 24/7, free parking garage, and no judgment at any hour.
